X/Twitter: most popular tweets as of 2023 The most liked tweet of all time was posted on American Actor Chadwick Boseman's Twitter account by his family members in August 2020 after his passing. As of January 2023, the post had seven million likes. Ranking second was a tweet by Elon Musk from April 2022, suggesting he would purchase Coca-Cola. Former U.S. President Barack Obama's 2017 tweet which stated "No one is born hating another person because of the color of his skin or his background or his religion..." has gained four million likes and 1.4 million retweets. Swedish environmental activist Greta Thunberg had two top ranking tweets as of January 2023. Ranking forth, a tweet from Thunberg which was published on December 27th 2022, was a response to American-British media personality Andrew Tate. Tate tweeted Greta and requested an email address to which he could submit a list of his collection of cars and each one's corresponding emissions, resulting in Thunberg's comeback gaining almost four million likes within a matter of days. Elon Musk Beats Out Rihanna For Most-Liked Tweet Of 2023 - Forbes A meme posted by Elon Musk on Twitter, the platform he owns, is the most-liked tweet so far this year, beating out Rihanna and Taylor Swift. Elon Musk Beats Out Rihanna For Most-Liked Tweet Of 2023 Topline A meme posted by Elon Musk on Twitter, the platform he owns, is the most-liked tweet so far this year, beating out Rihanna and Taylor Swift. Elon Musk has the most-liked tweet of 2023 so far. Copyright 2023 The Associated Press. All rights reserved. Key Facts The image—which reads, “people who have Twitter but never post anything”—features a man smoking and overlooking a vast field of people, who appear to be interacting with one another. Musk’s tweet received more than 1.5 million likes and over 128,000 retweets in just under a month (he posted it June 17). Rihanna—who tweeted a photo of her 1-year-old son RZA from a photoshoot with British Vogue—scored over 1.3 million likes. Another image posted by Musk featuring his dog Floki—who appears to be signing paperwork to become “the new CEO of Twitter”—is the third-most-liked tweet, followed by atweet from Swift announcing the re-recording of her 2010 album Speak Now, after the album’s release was teased by Swift during her “Eras Tour” earlier this year. Demar Hamlin, the Buffalo Bills player who suffered cardiac arrest during a regular season game in January, received over 1 million likes for a tweet four days after the incident saying he was “thankful for everyone who has reached out and prayed.” Most Likes Surprising Fact A March 17 tweet from Calvin Klein announcing a collaboration with BTS member Jungkook received the most retweets, with 329,922 retweets. Three of the other top five posts with the most retweets involved BTS, including two songannouncements and another tweet promoting Jungkook’s Calvin Klein collaboration.

This is the most liked tweet of 2021 - The Independent The most-liked tweet of the year was by President Joe Biden, after he was sworn in as the 46th president of the United States on 20 January. It was liked more than 4 million times and retweeted 486,000 times. Joe Biden broke former President Obama’s record for the most votes cast for a candidate in a presidential election. In 2008, Mr Obama received 69,498,516 votes, while President Joe Biden got 81,284,000 votes in the 2020 election. He got 306 electoral votes in total, 74 more than Donald Trump’s 232. The second most-liked tweet of 2021 was posted by South Korean singer and songwriter Jungkook from the K-pop group BTS. It was posted with a selfie and a kissing face emoji. The official Twitter amount tweeted “hello literally everyone” on 4 October 2021, which came in as the third most likes of the year, while fourth and fifth on the most-liked list were by Barack Obama and Kamala Harris, about the new presidency “Congratulations to my friend, President @JoeBiden! This is your time,” wrote former President Obama, and, “Ready to serve,” wrote Vice President Harris. The most retweeted tweet of the year was another one from the BTS official Twitter account, with the hashtags #StopAsianHate and #StopAAPIHate. The Korean mega pop band used their worldwide fame to back the anti-racism campaign. The message was retweeted nearly one million times. The most talked-about TV shows on Twitter included Brazillian reality TV shows Big Brother Brasil and A Fazenda 13, plus Korean Netflix series Squid Game, Wandavision and Japanese manga series Jujutsu Kaisen. Meanwhile, the list of most-discussed movies on Twitter in 2021 is dominated by action-adventure Superhero films including Black Widow, Black Panther, Eternals, Spider-Man No Way Home and Zack Snyder’s Justice League.

baseline.final_answer: Elon Musk posted the most-liked tweet of 2023 so far, a meme that received over 1.5 million likes. This tweet surpassed Rihanna's post about her son, which had over 1.3 million likes.

evaluation.feedback: The answer makes factual claims about the most-liked tweet of 2023, but provides no citations. The provided documents do not contain this information.
